meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome scaling hurdles. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first project.
content.answer_block: Scaling challenges often stem from misaligned value capture. By implementing a tiered SaaS pricing model, you shift from a 'free-tier' resource trap to a sustainable growth cycle. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing logic and Claude Code to integrate Stripe, ensuring your infrastructure grows alongside your user base.
content.intro:
The late-night coding sessions are over. You’ve shipped your first MVP, but the excitement is quickly replaced by a cold, hard realization: your infrastructure is buckling under the weight of free users, and your growth is hitting a ceiling. You didn't build a product; you built a hobby that costs money to run.

This isn't a failure—it's the most important milestone in a developer's career. Transitioning from a 'free-to-use' project to a professional SaaS requires a mental shift in how you view value. By mastering tiered pricing and automated billing, you stop paying for your users' success and start investing in your own.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your pricing tiers based on usage intensity rather than feature lists. This prevents 'feature bloat' and ensures you target the right users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Hobby (rate-limited), Pro (standard), and Enterprise (custom). Focus on usage-based limits like API requests per month.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate database schema for Stripe-compatible subscription tiers'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Gateway
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for Stripe Checkout. This keeps your payment logic decoupled from your core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular Stripe integration service in TypeScript. Include methods for creating checkout sessions and handling webhook events for subscription updates.

steps.title: Implement Usage Tracking
steps.description: To scale, you must track what users consume. This step creates a middleware that checks usage limits before allowing requests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks a user's current subscription tier against their monthly usage count stored in Redis.

steps.title: Automate Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Ensure your database stays in sync with Stripe. Automated webhooks prevent manual reconciliation errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust webhook handler for Stripe events: customer.subscription.created, customer.subscription.updated, and customer.subscription.deleted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create express webhook route for Stripe event validation'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Set up your environment variables and deploy. Monitoring your conversion rate is as important as monitoring your uptime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a Node.js SaaS application, including environment variable security and error logging configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --exec 'npm run build && npm run start'

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Pro' annual discount; it boosts immediate cash flow and reduces churn.
Never hardcode pricing logic in the frontend; always validate on the server side to prevent request tampering.
Use a 'usage-based' limit (like token count) instead of 'feature-gating' to keep your code DRY.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database so users don't lose access the second a payment fails.
Store your Stripe Price IDs in environment variables, not in your database, to make switching plans easier during testing.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on building an analytics dashboard to track your MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue). This will turn your project from a simple app into a data-driven business.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn your code into a sustainable business. Don't let your project stay a prototype—start charging for the value you create. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
